Question
What are the key factors affecting reserve balances in the Federal Reserve statistical release?
Answer

The Federal Reserve's H.4.1 statistical release details the factors affecting reserve balances of depository institutions. Here are the key components:

Factors Supplying Reserve Funds:

  • Securities held outright — The largest component, including U.S. Treasury securities, federal agency debt securities, and mortgage-backed securities
  • Loans — Including primary, secondary, and seasonal credit extended to depository institutions
  • Repurchase agreements — Securities purchased under agreements to resell
  • Float and other Federal Reserve assets — Items in process of collection and other balance sheet items

Factors Absorbing Reserve Funds:

  • Currency in circulation — Physical currency held outside the Federal Reserve
  • Reverse repurchase agreements — Including those with foreign official and international accounts
  • Deposits with Federal Reserve Banks — Including the U.S. Treasury general account and other deposits

The difference between supplying and absorbing factors determines the reserve balances with Federal Reserve Banks, which is a critical measure of liquidity available to depository institutions.
